prompt.content: Given the facts and timing surrounding Anthony Porter’s termination, as set forth in his personnel file, to what extent would SB 497 influence any determination regarding whether SBP violated section 6310 of the California Labor Code when it terminated Anthony?

rubrics.name: States that SB 497 would not influence any determination regarding whether SBP... (ver_1aee4388250442c4b6d04afed7436994)

messages.content: States that SB 497 would not influence any determination regarding whether SBP violated section 6310 of the California Labor Code.

golden_responses.golden_text_response:
SB 497 would not influence any determination regarding whether SBP violated section 6310 of the California Labor Code.
SB 497 describes itself as “[a]n act to amend Sections 98.6, 1102.5, and 1197.5 of the Labor Code.” It revised those specific sections to add a rebuttable presumption of retaliation for prohibited employer conduct that occurs within 90 days of a protected activity. For example, the following language was added to section 98.6: “If an employer engages in any action prohibited by this section within 90 days of the protected activity specified in this section, there shall be a rebuttable presumption in favor of the employee’s claim.”

However, the prompt is asking about a potential violation of section 6310 of the Labor Code, and that section was not revised by SB 497. Accordingly, the rebuttable presumption does not apply, and SB 497 does not alter the analysis.
